core_mechanism: extreme-value (Gumbel) statistics for the maximum of a large collection of random distances, where the maximum's tail follows a double-exponential law with shape/scale/location parameters set by the exponential (branching) growth of the number of elements at a given distance
brief_reason: The eccentricity is the maximum shortest-path distance from a node, and its tail distribution takes the double-exponential Gumbel form 1 - exp[-exp(-(e^{bl}-mu)/beta)] with a shape parameter b = ln(c-1) reflecting exponential shell growth, i.e. the classic extreme-value law for a maximum over many samples.
